preamble: The phonetic transformation from the alveolar lateral approximant /l/ to the voiceless postalveolar affricate /tʃ/ is primarily attested through processes of assimilation and palatalization, often occurring within consonant clusters.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves a change in the manner of articulation from an approximant to an affricate, a change in the place of articulation from alveolar to postalveolar, and typically includes palatalization and assimilation to an adjacent postalveolar sound

examples.note: Assimilation of the definite article /al-/ to a following /tʃ/.
Evolution of Latin /CL/ cluster to /tʃ/ through palatalization.

sources: Elramli, Y. M. (2011). Assimilation in the Phonology of a Libyan Arabic Dialect: A Constraint-Based Approach. Newcastle University.
Recasens, D. (2025). The diachronic evolution of syllable-onset /Cl/ clusters in Romance revisited.
Hall, R. A. (1976). From Latin to Romance in Sound Charts. Cornell University Press.
